query: I generated 100K synthetic credit-card transactions using a Bayesian network I trained on internal data. There's no upstream URL — everything came from my generator. Audit my source provenance.
expected_behavior: Does NOT engage the upstream-URL / source_repo / source_sha workflow that applies to externally-ingested data
Explains that for synthetic data, provenance is the generator code SHA + the seed + the training data's own provenance (transitively), and names those fields explicitly
Hands off (or notes the relevant adjacent skill) for synthetic-data utility / leakage audits, rather than forcing the ingest-time provenance schema onto a generator-produced dataset
